title: Interview Essentials
description: The words, phrases, and conversations you need to walk into any interview with confidence.
description_sw: Maneno, misemo, na mazungumzo unayohitaji kuingia mahojiano yoyote kwa ujasiri.
sofia_intro: Module 7 is your interview toolkit. These are the words recruiters use every single day — and now you will use them too.
sofia_intro_sw: Moduli ya 7 ni kifaa chako cha mahojiano. Haya ndio maneno ambayo waajiri hutumia kila siku — na sasa wewe pia utayatumia.

lessons.title: Interview Vocabulary
lessons.sofia_hint: Click each word to hear it. These are the words every recruiter expects you to know.
lessons.sofia_hint_sw: Bonyeza kila neno kulisikia. Haya ndio maneno ambayo kila mwajiri anatarajia ujue.

words.example: I have five years of experience.
I work on my weaknesses every day.

lessons.title: Interview Word Match
lessons.sofia_hint: Pick the correct word for each clue. No writing — just click!
lessons.sofia_hint_sw: Chagua neno sahihi kwa kila kidokezo. Hakuna kuandika — bonyeza tu!

questions.prompt: 'My strength is teamwork' — what does 'strength' mean here?
questions.prompt_sw: 'My strength is teamwork' — 'strength' inamaanisha nini hapa?

lessons.title: Interview Dialogues
lessons.sofia_hint: These are real interview conversations. Listen carefully and repeat each line.
lessons.sofia_hint_sw: Hizi ni mazungumzo halisi ya mahojiano. Sikiliza kwa makini na ujibu kila mstari.

lines.speaker: Recruiter
lines.en: Good morning. Thank you for coming in today.
lines.sw: Habari za asubuhi. Asante kwa kuja leo.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Good morning. Thank you for having me.
lines.sw: Habari za asubuhi. Asante kwa kunikaribisho.

lines.speaker: Recruiter
lines.en: Please tell me a little about yourself.
lines.sw: Tafadhali nipe maelezo kidogo kuhusu wewe.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I have three years of experience in customer service and I am very motivated to grow.
lines.sw: Nina miaka mitatu ya uzoefu kwenye huduma za wateja na niko na motisha kubwa kukua.

lines.speaker: Recruiter
lines.sw: Nguvu yako kubwa zaidi ni nini?

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: My biggest strength is communication. I listen carefully and respond clearly.
lines.sw: Nguvu yangu kubwa zaidi ni mawasiliano. Nasikiliza kwa makini na najibu wazi.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I sometimes focus too much on details, but I am learning to balance speed and quality.
lines.sw: Wakati mwingine ninajikita sana kwenye maelezo, lakini ninajifunza kuweka usawa kati ya kasi na ubora.

lines.speaker: Recruiter
lines.en: Do you have any questions for us?

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Yes. What does a typical day look like in this role?
lines.sw: Ndiyo. Siku ya kawaida inaonekanaje katika jukumu hili?

lines.speaker: Recruiter
lines.en: Great question. We will email you the details. Thank you for your time.
lines.sw: Swali zuri. Tutakutumia maelezo kwa barua pepe. Asante kwa muda wako.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Thank you. I look forward to hearing from you.
lines.sw: Asante. Ninasubiri kupata habari kutoka kwenu.

exercises.transcript: We are looking for someone with at least two years of experience and strong communication skills.
exercises.transcript_sw: Tunatafuta mtu aliye na uzoefu wa miaka miwili au zaidi na ujuzi imara wa mawasiliano.
exercises.question: What is one requirement?
exercises.question_sw: Sehemu moja ya mahitaji ni ipi?

exercises.transcript: The salary for this position starts at forty thousand shillings per month, with a review after three months.
exercises.transcript_sw: Mshahara wa nafasi hii huanza shilingi arobaini elfu kwa mwezi, na tathmini baada ya miezi mitatu.
exercises.question: When is the salary reviewed?

exercises.transcript: We value teamwork, punctuality, and a positive attitude above all else.
exercises.transcript_sw: Tuthamini ushirikiano wa timu, kufika kwa wakati, na mtazamo mzuri kuliko yote.
exercises.question: Which quality is NOT mentioned?

exercises.transcript: Please bring two copies of your resume and a valid ID to the interview.
exercises.transcript_sw: Tafadhali lete nakala mbili za wasifu wako na kitambulisho halali kwenye mahojiano.
exercises.question: How many copies of the resume should you bring?
exercises.question_sw: Unapaswa kuleta nakala ngapi za wasifu?

lessons.title: Workplace Phrases and Abbreviations
lessons.title_sw: Misemo na Vifupisho vya Mahali pa Kazi
lessons.sofia_hint: These short phrases are used in every office. Click to hear them!
lessons.sofia_hint_sw: Misemo hii mifupi inatumika katika kila ofisi. Bonyeza kuisikia!

words.example: FYI: the meeting moved to 3 PM.
I will follow up on Monday.
Your action item is the report.
I will circle back after the meeting.
I do not have the bandwidth right now.

lessons.title: Workplace Phrases Quiz
lessons.sofia_hint: Pick the correct phrase or meaning. Short answers only!
lessons.sofia_hint_sw: Chagua msemo au maana sahihi. Majibu mafupi tu!

questions.prompt: 'FYI: the meeting moved' — what does FYI mean?
questions.prompt_sw: 'FYI: the meeting moved' — FYI inamaanisha nini?

questions.prompt: 'I do not have the bandwidth right now' means:
questions.prompt_sw: 'I do not have the bandwidth right now' inamaanisha:

questions.prompt: 'My availability starts Monday' — what does availability mean?
Best way to close an interview:
When a recruiter says 'Tell me about your weakness', the best answer mentions:
questions.prompt_sw: Mwajiri akisema 'Nieleze kuhusu udhaifu wako', jibu bora linataja:
options: A weakness and how you improve it

questions.prompt: 'Please bring two copies of your resume' — how many copies?
questions.prompt_sw: 'Please bring two copies of your resume' — nakala ngapi?
